snp2.9:1.2:
324
“Having what good behavior,
having what sort of conduct,
fostering what kind of actions,
can a person be properly settled
and attain the highest goal?” (1)
snp2.9:1.3:
325
“One should be respectful toward elders, without envy;
one should know the proper time for seeing one’s teachers.
When a Dhamma talk is occurring, one who knows the occasion
should listen carefully to well-spoken words. (2)
snp2.9:1.4:
326
“One should go to teachers at the proper time,
having dropped one’s pride, with a humble manner. [57]
One should recollect and practice the meaning,
the Dhamma, self-control, and the spiritual life. (3)
snp2.9:1.5:
327
“Delighting in the Dhamma, delighted with the Dhamma,
firm in the Dhamma, knowing how to judge the Dhamma,
one should not engage in talk that corrupts the Dhamma
but should be guided by genuine well-spoken words. (4)
snp2.9:1.6:
328
“Having abandoned laughter, chatter, lamenting,
annoyance, hypocritical deeds, scheming, greed, conceit,
stubbornness, crudeness, blemishes, infatuation:
one should live free from vanity, inwardly firm. (5)
snp2.9:1.8:
330
“Those who delight in the Dhamma taught by the noble ones
are unsurpassed in speech, mind, and action.
Being settled in peace, gentleness, and concentration,
they have reached the core of learning and wisdom.” (7)
